author: deme1744
content: Any update on this? Do we go with not accounting those EIP7997 related accesses as per current spec? Do other clients agree with this?

author: marioevz
content:
I think this should be discussed in ACD at some point. Just adding this lookup at the fork boundary requires some refactoring in EELS for us to be able to tell the T8N that it's the first block of the fork, and it's not worth it in my opinion.

I would prefer that this EIP becomes completely informational and the irregular state transition suggestion is removed, sort of like a warning that if your chain is starting at amsterdam fork from genesis, or transition to it, you need to make sure that the pre-allocation contains this contract at the correct address.

author: deme1744
content: Yes, I don't mind that much whether or not we add the irregular state transition (although in general having those accesses in the witness is rather useless, so I'd prefer not to have those) , but at least your PR makes it unambiguously clear how it should work. And brings spec and EIP in line.
